description:
## Question

What do the supported Tauri APIs and Beadwork CLI actually guarantee for native directory picking, durable local storage, and validating that a chosen path is the root of a Beadwork-initialized Git repository? Record an evidence-backed markdown summary as a linked asset, including API versions, error behavior, and implications for the workspace specification.

comments.text:
## Resolution

The evidence is recorded in [workspace-picker-store-and-validation.md](docs/research/workspace-picker-store-and-validation.md).

Tauri 2.11.3 can use the dialog picker and backend-owned store plugins. The switcher must normalize a selected directory to the Beadwork Git root: bw config list succeeds from subdirectories, so success alone does not make the selected path a Workspace. Validate through the CLI, resolve the canonical root independently, then deduplicate, load, and persist the root path. Explicit store save belongs in the successful switch commit; failed persistence retains the old Current Workspace.

title: Research workspace picker, store, and Beadwork validation capabilities
